### United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coal_mining&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: United States")]

Main article: [Coal mining in the United States](/wiki/Coal_mining_in_the_United_States "Coal mining in the United States")

Further information: [List of coal mines in the United States](/wiki/List_of_coal_mines_in_the_United_States "List of coal mines in the United States") and [Coal towns](/wiki/Coal_towns "Coal towns")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/61/GROUP_OF_MINERS_WAITING_TO_GO_TO_WORK_ON_THE_4_P.M._TO_MIDNIGHT_SHIFT_AT_THE_VIRGINIA-POCAHONTAS_COAL_COMPANY_MINE_%5E4..._-_NARA_-_556348.jpg/250px-GROUP_OF_MINERS_WAITING_TO_GO_TO_WORK_ON_THE_4_P.M._TO_MIDNIGHT_SHIFT_AT_THE_VIRGINIA-POCAHONTAS_COAL_COMPANY_MINE_%5E4..._-_NARA_-_556348.jpg)](/wiki/File:GROUP_OF_MINERS_WAITING_TO_GO_TO_WORK_ON_THE_4_P.M._TO_MIDNIGHT_SHIFT_AT_THE_VIRGINIA-POCAHONTAS_COAL_COMPANY_MINE_%5E4..._-_NARA_-_556348.jpg)

Miners at the Virginia-Pocahontas Coal Company Mine in 1974 waiting to go to work on the 4 pm to midnight shift

Coal began being mined in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") in the early 18th century, and commercial mining started around 1730 in [Midlothian, Virginia](/wiki/Midlothian,_Virginia "Midlothian, Virginia").[[127]](#cite_note-127)
The U.S. share of world coal production remained steady at about 20 percent from 1980 to 2005, at about 1 billion [short tons](/wiki/Short_tons "Short tons") per year. The United States was ranked as the [second](/wiki/List_of_countries_by_coal_production "List of countries by coal production") highest coal producing country in the world in 2010, and possesses the largest [coal reserves](/wiki/Coal_reserves "Coal reserves") in the world. In 2008 then-[President George W. Bush](/wiki/President_George_W._Bush "President George W. Bush") stated that coal was the most reliable source of electricity.[[128]](#cite_note-128)

In 2011, U.S. president [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama") said that the U.S. should rely more on cleaner sources of energy that emit lower or no [carbon dioxide pollution](/wiki/Greenhouse_gas "Greenhouse gas").[[129]](#cite_note-129) For a time, while domestic coal consumption for electric power was being displaced by natural gas, exports grew.[[130]](#cite_note-130) U.S. net coal exports increased ninefold from 2006 to 2012, peaked at 117 million short tons in 2012, then declined to 63 million tons in 2015. In 2015, 60% of net US exports went to Europe, 27% to Asia. US coal production increasingly comes from strip mines in the western United States, such as from the [Powder River Basin](/wiki/Powder_River_Basin "Powder River Basin") in [Wyoming](/wiki/Wyoming "Wyoming") and [Montana](/wiki/Montana "Montana").[[14]](#cite_note-T1-14)[[131]](#cite_note-SFC031713-131)

Coal has come under continued price pressure from [natural gas](/wiki/Natural_gas "Natural gas") and [renewable energy](/wiki/Renewable_energy "Renewable energy"), which has resulted in a rapid decline of coal in the U.S. and several notable bankruptcies including [Peabody Energy](/wiki/Peabody_Energy "Peabody Energy"). On 13 April 2016 the company reported that its revenue had reduced by 17 percent as coal prices fell and that it had lost two billion dollars the previous year.[[132]](#cite_note-:0-132) It then filed [Chapter 11 bankruptcy](/wiki/Chapter_11_bankruptcy_protection "Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection") on 13 April 2016.[[132]](#cite_note-:0-132) *[Harvard Business Review](/wiki/Harvard_Business_Review "Harvard Business Review")* addressed retraining coal workers for [solar photovoltaic](/wiki/Solar_photovoltaic "Solar photovoltaic") employment because of the rapid rise in U.S. solar jobs.[[133]](#cite_note-133) A 2016 study indicated that this was technically possible and would account for only 5% of the industrial revenue from a single year to provide coal workers with job security in the energy industry as a whole.[[32]](#cite_note-Louie-32)

[Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") pledged to bring back coal jobs during the [2016 US presidential election](/wiki/2016_US_presidential_election "2016 US presidential election"), and as president he announced plans to reduce environmental protection, particularly by repealing the [Clean Power Plan](/wiki/Clean_Power_Plan "Clean Power Plan") (CPP). However, industry observers have warned that this might not lead to a boom in mining jobs.[[134]](#cite_note-134)

A 2019 projection by the [Energy Information Administration](/wiki/Energy_Information_Administration "Energy Information Administration") estimated that coal production without CPP would decline over coming decades at a faster rate than indicated in the agency's 2017 projection, which had assumed the CPP was in effect.[[135]](#cite_note-135)

### Vietnam

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coal_mining&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Vietnam")]

The [Quang Yen coalfield](/wiki/Quang_Yen_coalfield "Quang Yen coalfield") was discovered in Vietnam in the 1880s.[[136]](#cite_note-136) [Vinacomin](/wiki/Vinacomin "Vinacomin") estimates coal reserves in Vietnam at 50 billion tons, concentrated in the Northeastern basin and the [Red River Delta coalfield](/wiki/Red_River_Delta_coalfield "Red River Delta coalfield").[[137]](#cite_note-137)
